title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: bayescoveragemodel R package fits Bayesian hierarchical transition models health coverage indicators (ANC4, institutional delivery, vaccination) using survey routine data. uses Stan via cmdstanr Bayesian estimation.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: read, modify, reference files /private folder.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Load package for development devtools::load_all()  # Generate/update roxygen documentation devtools::document()  # Build the package devtools::build()  # Check the package devtools::check()  # Generate Stan model from templates write_model(add_aggregates = FALSE, add_routine = FALSE)  # Compile Stan model compile_model(model_name = "spline", threads_per_chain = 1)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/ - Core package functions (fit_model.R main entry point) inst/stan/ - Stan model files templates (uses {{}} placeholders) data_raw/ - Raw public input data (surveys, regional metadata) data/ - Processed data saved model summaries vignettes/ - Package documentation tutorials man/ - Auto-generated roxygen documentation

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Stan models generated templates via write_model(): - Templates use {{placeholder}} syntax - Combines building blocks inst/stan/ - Produces 4 variants: fpem, fpem_routine, fpem_aggregates, fpem_routine_aggregates

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package uses multiple scales delta method transformations: - probit(), inv_probit(), logit(), inv_logit() - SE helpers: get_se_logitprop(), get_se_invprobitprop()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: process_data() - Prepare data (filter, transform, assign outliers) explore_data() - Generate EDA plots fit_model() - Fit runstep parameter (“step1a”, “step1ab”, “step1b”, “local_national”) plot_estimates_local_all() - Visualize results

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Model outputs stored ../bayestransition_output/ (one level repo). Use get_output_dir() construct paths.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: exported functions roxygen documentation #' comments Uses tidyverse conventions (dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2) Function parameters use snake_case Hierarchical structures defined via character vectors (e.g., c("intercept", "name_region", "name_country"))

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Stan compilation may fail folder paths contain spaces Model objects class "fpemplus" nooutlier column uses inverted logic: 1=outlier, 0=possible outlier

title: Model Diagnostics
code: library(bayescoveragemodel) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(localhierarchy)

title: Model Diagnostics
text: Load previously fitted models diagnostics:
code: # Choose indicator and model type indicator <- "anc4" model_name <- "spline"  # or "rw2"  # Load step 1ab fit (main global model) fit <- get_fit(indicator, runstep = "step1ab", folder_suffix = "run_workflow")  # Load validation fit fit_val <- get_fit(indicator, runstep = "step1ab", folder_suffix = "val_workflow")

title: Model Diagnostics
text: Create plots countries visually assess model fit:
code: # Plot estimates from main fit plots_main <- plot_estimates_local_all(   results = fit,   save_plots = TRUE,   indicator_name = indicator )  # Compare main fit vs validation plots_comparison <- plot_estimates_local_all(   results = fit,   results2 = fit_val,   modelnames = c("Main", "Validation"),   save_plots = TRUE,   indicator_name = indicator )

title: Model Diagnostics
text: Check MCMC convergence using standard diagnostics: Key diagnostics check: Rhat: close 1.0 (< 1.01 good) ESS (Effective Sample Size): sufficiently large
code: # Get convergence diagnostics diag <- get_convergence_diagnostics(fit) print(diag)

title: Model Diagnostics
text: model parameters modeled hierarchically. can check parameters follows: plots help assess: Prior-posterior comparison hierarchical hyperparameters Shrinkage behavior across groups
code: # Plot hierarchical checks plot_hierchecks(fit)

title: Model Diagnostics
text: Compare estimates different model runs (e.g., spline vs RW2):
code: # Load both model types fit_spline <- get_fit(indicator, runstep = "step1ab", folder_suffix = "run_spline") fit_rw2 <- get_fit(indicator, runstep = "step1ab", folder_suffix = "run_rw2")  # Compare spline and RW2 models plots_model_comparison <- plot_estimates_local_all(   results = fit_spline,   results2 = fit_rw2,   modelnames = c("Spline", "RW2"),   save_plots = TRUE,   indicator_name = indicator )

title: Global Model Fitting
code: library(bayescoveragemodel) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(haven) library(cmdstanr) library(localhierarchy)

title: Global Model Fitting
code: # Read national survey data and region metadata data_folder <- "data_raw" dat0 <- read_dta(here::here(data_folder, "ICEH_national.dta")) regions_dat <- readr::read_csv(   here::here(data_folder, "regions_updated.csv"))  # Choose an indicator: "anc4", "ideliv", or "vdpt" indicator_select <- "anc4"  # Process data dat <- process_data(   dat = dat0,   regions_dat = regions_dat,   indicator = indicator_select )

title: Global Model Fitting
text: primary workflow uses step 1ab fit global model:
code: fit1ab <- fit_model(   runstep = "step1ab",   survey_df = dat,   y = "invprobit_indicator",   se = "se_invprobit_indicator",   get_posteriors = TRUE,   chains = 4 )

title: Global Model Fitting
text: Step 1a can run separately extract residuals model checking.
code: fit1a <- fit_model(   runstep = "step1a",   survey_df = dat,   y = "invprobit_indicator",   se = "se_invprobit_indicator",   get_posteriors = TRUE,   chains = 4 )  # Extract residuals residuals <- get_residuals_samples(fit = fit1a)

title: Global Model Fitting
text: global fitting, run local models countries obtain final estimates:
code: fit_local_global <- fit_model(   runstep = "local_national",   y = "invprobit_indicator",   se = "se_invprobit_indicator",   survey_df = dat,   save_post_summ = TRUE,   get_posteriors = TRUE,   chains = 4 )

title: Global Model Fitting
code: # Plot estimates for all countries p <- plot_estimates_local_all(   results = fit_local_global,   save_plots = TRUE,   indicator_name = indicator_select )

title: Global Model Fitting
text: Model fits stored bayestransitionoutput folder one level project root. Use get_fit() load saved fits:
code: # Load a previously saved fit fit <- get_fit(   indicator = "anc4",   runstep = "step1b",   folder_suffix = "run_workflow" )

title: Global Model Fitting
text: --Sample Validation: Assess predictive performance Model Diagnostics: Check convergence model fit

title: Out-of-Sample Validation
code: library(bayescoveragemodel) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(haven) library(cmdstanr) library(localhierarchy)

title: Out-of-Sample Validation
code: # Read data data_folder <- "data_raw" dat0 <- read_dta(here::here(data_folder, "ICEH_national.dta")) regions_dat <- readr::read_csv(   here::here(data_folder, "regions_updated.csv"))  # Choose indicator indicator_select <- "anc4"  # Process data dat <- process_data(   dat = dat0,   regions_dat = regions_dat,   indicator = indicator_select )

title: Out-of-Sample Validation
text: Choose cutoff year validation. Data start_date >= validation_cutoff_year held validation.
code: # Set validation cutoff year validation_cutoff_year <- 2018  # Check percentage of data held out prop_held_out <- mean(dat$start_date >= validation_cutoff_year) cat("Proportion of data held out:", round(prop_held_out, 3), "\n")

title: Out-of-Sample Validation
code: fit_val <- fit_model(   runstep = "step1ab",   survey_df = dat,   y = "invprobit_indicator",   se = "se_invprobit_indicator",   get_posteriors = TRUE,   chains = 4,   validation_cutoff_year = validation_cutoff_year )

title: Out-of-Sample Validation
text: Plot estimates see well model predicts held-data: plots show: Fitted estimates using training data (cutoff) Held-observations (cutoff) visual comparison Results can analysed using bayescoveragemodelchecks package.
code: plots_val <- plot_estimates_local_all(   results = fit_val,   save_plots = TRUE,   indicator_name = indicator_select )

code: @Manual{,   title = {bayescoveragemodel: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators},   author = {Leontine Alkema and Shauna Mooney},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1},   url = {https://alkemalab.github.io/bayescoveragemodel/}, }

what: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
title: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
text: R package fit Bayesian hierarchical transition models health coverage indicators (e.g., ANC4, institutional delivery) using survey routine data. Implements models using Stan Bayesian inference, support global local (country-specific) model fitting, --sample validation, subnational estimation. work supported, whole part, Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation (INV-001299).

title: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
text: Dependencies cmdstanr: Instructions installing cmdstanr available Getting started guide. R package localhierarchy, available github.com/AlkemaLab/localhierarchy. can install using remotes::install_github("AlkemaLab/localhierarchy") Install bayescoveragemodel Github: remotes::install_github("AlkemaLab/bayescoveragemodel")

title: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
text: demonstrate quick example workflow fitting Bayesian transition models health coverage indicators. use minimal sampling iterations demonstration purposes. full model fitting proper sampling settings, see Global Model Fitting article.

title: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
code: library(bayescoveragemodel) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
text: package includes example survey data health coverage indicators (ANC4, institutional delivery, vaccination).
code: # Load survey data data_folder <- system.file("extdata", package = "bayescoveragemodel")  # If using development version with data_raw folder if (data_folder == "") {   data_folder <- "data_raw" }  # Load regions metadata regions_dat <- readr::read_csv(   file.path(data_folder, "regions_updated.csv"),   show_col_types = FALSE )

title: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
text: Use process_data() prepare survey data model fitting.
code: # Load raw survey data (example using haven for .dta files) dat0 <- haven::read_dta(file.path(data_folder, "ICEH_national.dta"))  # Process data for a specific indicator dat <- process_data(   dat = dat0,   regions_dat = regions_dat,   indicator = "anc4" )  head(dat)

title: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
text: fitting models, explore survey data using explore_data().
code: # Create exploration plots plots <- explore_data(  data = dat,   indicator_col = "indicator",   group_col = "country",   data_types_col = "data_series_type",   indicator_name = "ANC4",   region_col = "cluster" )  # View one of the plots plots[[1]]

title: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
text: Fit model using test settings minimal iterations. runs quickly used inference.
code: # Write Stan models (run once) write_model()  # Fit model with test settings (fast, not for inference) fit <- fit_model(   runstep = "step1ab",   survey_df = dat,   y = "invprobit_indicator",   se = "se_invprobit_indicator",   runnumber = "test_run",   # Model defaults to "spline" (a Bayesian transition model), "rw2" argument can be used for fitting an ARIMA(1,2,0) model.    model_name = "spline",    chains = 2,   # Test settings for quick demonstration   iter_sampling = 10,   iter_warmup = 5,   get_posteriors = TRUE )

title: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
text: Visualize fitted estimates.
code: # Plot estimates for all countries p <- plot_estimates_local_all(   results = fit,   save_plots = FALSE,   indicator_name = "anc4" )  # Display a plot p[[1]]

title: Bayesian Transition Models for Estimating and Forecasting Health Coverage Indicators
text: production model fitting proper sampling: Global Model Fitting: See Global Model Fitting article complete workflow recommended sampling settings. Validation: See --Sample Validation article assessing predictive performance. Diagnostics: See Model Diagnostics article convergence checks hierarchical model assessment.

what: Add Probit Scale Transformation Columns — add_probit_scale_columns
title: Add Probit Scale Transformation Columns — add_probit_scale_columns
text: Adds standardized columns probit/proportion scale transformations. Used get_residuals_samples get_inno_samples ensure consistent column naming transformations.

title: Add Probit Scale Transformation Columns — add_probit_scale_columns
code: add_probit_scale_columns(   df,   eta_col,   sd_col = NULL,   sd_value = 1,   y_col = NULL )

title: Add Probit Scale Transformation Columns — add_probit_scale_columns
text: df Data frame transform eta_col Name column containing eta values probit scale sd_col Name column containing standard deviation probit scale, NULL use sd_value sd_value Numeric value sd_y sd_col NULL (default: 1) y_col Name column containing y values proportion scale, NULL y computed residual + yhat

title: Add Probit Scale Transformation Columns — add_probit_scale_columns
text: Data frame added columns: level_prop: Eta probit scale level: Coverage proportion scale yhat: Fitted value proportion scale (level) sd_y: Standard deviation probit scale sd_y_prop: Standard deviation proportion scale y_prop: y probit scale y_col NULL residual column exists, also adds: y: Reconstructed observation (residual + yhat)

title: Add uncertainty to observations — add_uncertainty_in_obs
text: function adds uncertainty observations using model fit object.

title: Add uncertainty to observations — add_uncertainty_in_obs
code: add_uncertainty_in_obs(fit, perc_low = 0.025, perc_up = 0.975)

title: Add uncertainty to observations — add_uncertainty_in_obs
text: fit model_fit object. perc_low lower percentile uncertainty. perc_up upper percentile uncertainty.

what: Assign which observations are modeled with an outlier error term — assign_outliers
title: Assign which observations are modeled with an outlier error term — assign_outliers
text: Determines observations assigned outlier error model fit. Uses either pre-specified outlier indicators data, algorithm based observation year, data series type, country.

title: Assign which observations are modeled with an outlier error term — assign_outliers
code: assign_outliers(dat, outlier_record_ids = NULL)

title: Assign which observations are modeled with an outlier error term — assign_outliers
text: dat data frame survey observations. May contain columns: possible_outlier Binary indicator observation possible outlier possible_outlier_userinput User override possible_outlier record_id_fixed Unique observation identifier (required outlier_record_ids provided) data_series_type Type data series (e.g., "DHS", "National survey", "") any_bias Binary indicator known biases iso Country ISO code year Observation year outlier_record_ids Optional vector record_id_fixed values flag possible outliers. Typically obtained step 1a fit using identify_outliers_in_global_fit.

title: Assign which observations are modeled with an outlier error term — assign_outliers
text: input data frame additional column nooutlier (1 = outlier, 0 = possible outlier gets outlier error term).

title: Assign which observations are modeled with an outlier error term — assign_outliers
text: function uses following priority determining outliers: possible_outlier_userinput provided, use possible_outlier column exists, use Otherwise, apply algorithm: DHS surveys 1990, observations known biases, flagged possible outliers

what: Back-transform Residuals to Proportion Scale — backtransform_residuals
title: Back-transform Residuals to Proportion Scale — backtransform_residuals
text: Transforms residuals probit scale back original proportion scale. Computes response probit scale, calculates residuals standard errors proportion scale.

title: Back-transform Residuals to Proportion Scale — backtransform_residuals
code: backtransform_residuals(df)

title: Back-transform Residuals to Proportion Scale — backtransform_residuals
text: df Data frame containing columns: level: Predicted value probit scale residual: Residual probit scale scale: Standard error probit scale

title: Back-transform Residuals to Proportion Scale — backtransform_residuals
text: Data frame added columns: response: Observed value probit scale (level + residual) residual_ori: Residual proportion scale scale_ori: Standard error proportion scale

what: Compare multiple model fits in a single plot — compare_fits
title: Compare multiple model fits in a single plot — compare_fits
text: function takes run names, loads corresponding fits, plots together using plot_estimates_local_all.

title: Compare multiple model fits in a single plot — compare_fits
code: compare_fits(   runnames,   modelnames = NULL,   iso_codes = NULL,   dat_routine = NULL,   indicator_name = NULL,   save_plots = FALSE,   output_folder = NULL,   add_caption = FALSE,   add_estimates = TRUE,   use_for_facetting = FALSE )

title: Compare multiple model fits in a single plot — compare_fits
text: runnames Character vector run names (2-4 names). Run names follow pattern: indicatorrunstepfolder_suffix, e.g., "anc4_step1b_run_alldata_jan16" modelnames Character vector display names legend (length runnames). NULL, uses runnames. iso_codes Optional character vector ISO codes filter plots. NULL, returns plots countries. dat_routine Optional routine data overlay plots indicator_name Custom title y-axis (NULL, pulled fit$data) save_plots Boolean save plots PDF output_folder Override directory saving add_caption Boolean add explanatory caption add_estimates Boolean show model estimates/ribbons use_for_facetting Boolean adjust plot titles facetted output

title: Compare multiple model fits in a single plot — compare_fits
text: List ggplot objects, one per geographic unit (filtered iso_codes provided)

title: Compare multiple model fits in a single plot — compare_fits
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Compare step1a and step1b for anc4 plots <- compare_fits(   runnames = c("anc4_step1b_run_alldata_jan16", "anc4_step1a_run_alldata_jan16"),   modelnames = c("Step 1b", "Step 1a") )  # Compare validation and non-validation fits for specific countries plots <- compare_fits(   runnames = c("vdpt_step1b_run_alldata_jan16", "vdpt_step1b_val_alldata_jan16"),   modelnames = c("Full model", "Validation"),   iso_codes = c("ETH", "GHA", "KEN", "NGA", "UGA") ) } # }

what: Explore Coverage Indicators Over Time — explore_data
title: Explore Coverage Indicators Over Time — explore_data

code: explore_data(   data,   indicator_col,   indicator_se_col = NULL,   data_types_col = NULL,   group_col,   region_col = NULL,   indicator_name = NULL,   routine_data = NULL )

title: Explore Coverage Indicators Over Time — explore_data
text: data data frame containing coverage indicators year variable. indicator_col string specifying column name coverage indicator. indicator_se_col optional string specifying column name standard error indicator. data_types_col optional string specifying column name data types data frame. group_col string specifying column name first-level grouping (e.g., country). region_col optional string specifying column name second-level grouping (e.g., region). indicator_name optional string use title plots instead default "Indicator". routine_data optional data frame containing routine data.

title: Explore Coverage Indicators Over Time — explore_data
text: series plots exploratory data analysis.

what: Fit the transition model to data. — fit_model
title: Fit the transition model to data. — fit_model
text: Fit transition model data.

title: Fit the transition model to data. — fit_model
code: fit_model(   survey_df,   national_dat_df = NULL,   routine_df = NULL,   mean_log_sigma = NULL,   popweights = NULL,   y = "invprobit_indicator",   se = "se_invprobit_indicator",   year = "year",   source = "data_series_type",   area = "iso",   iso_select = NULL,   routine_data = NULL,   start_year = 2000,   end_year = 2030,   runstep,   global_fit = NULL,   t_star = 2010,   spline_degree = 2,   num_knots = 8,   Betas_upper_bound = 0.5,   Betas_lower_bound = 0.01,   Ptilde_low = 0,   add_dataoutliers = TRUE,   extra_stan_data = list(),   hierarchical_level = c("intercept", "cluster", "subcluster", "iso"),   hierarchical_splines = c("intercept", "cluster", "subcluster", "iso"),   hierarchical_asymptote = c("intercept", "iso"),   add_subnational_hierarchy = "admin1",   use_globalsubnat_fromnat = FALSE,   model_name = "spline",   held_out = FALSE,   validation_cutoff_year = NULL,   validation_run = FALSE,   save_post_summ = FALSE,   generate_quantities = TRUE,   get_posteriors = TRUE,   create_runname_and_outputdir = TRUE,   runnumber = 1,   rungroup = NULL,   runname = NULL,   chains = 4,   iter_sampling = 200,   iter_warmup = 150,   add_sample = TRUE,   compile_model = TRUE,   force_recompile = FALSE,   seed = 1234,   refresh = 10,   adapt_delta = 0.9,   max_treedepth = 14,   variational = FALSE,   nthreads_variational = 8,   add_inits = TRUE )

title: Fit the transition model to data. — fit_model
text: survey_df tibble survey data routine_df tibble routine data y column name outcome. se column name outcome standard error. year column name outcome year. source column name data source. area column name area observation subnational: iso_select ISO code use local national run start_year start year estimates. end_year end year estimates. Settings global model fit 1a runstep type run, currently one "step1a", "step1ab", "step1b", "local_national" (see Details). global_fit optional object class "fpemplus", used obtain fixed values use parameters current fit (see Details). t_star reference year used model. spline_degree spline degree. Degree 2 3 supported. num_knots number spline knots. Betas_upper_bound upper bound splines parameters Betas_lower_bound lower bound splines parameters Ptilde_low lower bound asymptote Ptilde add_dataoutliers boolean indicator whether include data outliers 1b extra_stan_data list additional data pass Stan model hierarchical_level vector specifying hierarchical structure level reference year (see Details). hierarchical_splines vector specifying hierarchical structure spline coefficients (see Details). hierarchical_asymptote vector specifying hierarchical structure asymptote (see Details). model_name character string specifying model fit. Options : "spline" (default): Transition model, ARIMA(1,1,0) spline-based drift. "rw2": Local rate change model, ARIMA(1,2,0) held_out binary vector indicating observations held . Set FALSE hold observations. validation_cutoff_year year use --sample validation, overwrites held_out (confirm ) validation_run boolean indicator whether validation model run generate_quantities binary vector indicating whether simulate data fitted model Setting save things get_posteriors boolean indicator whether return posterior samples create_runname_and_outputdir boolean indicator whether create runname output directory runnumber number add runname rungroup group add runname runname name use run Settings sampling chains number chains run iter_sampling number posterior samples draw iter_warmup number warmup iterations add_sample boolean indicator whether return samples compile_model boolean indicator whether compile Stan model force_recompile boolean indicator whether force recompilation Stan model seed random seed refresh number iterations progress updates adapt_delta target acceptance rate -U-Turn Sampler max_treedepth maximum tree depth -U-Turn Sampler variational boolean indicator whether use variational inference (yet tested) nthreads_variational number threads use variational inference add_inits boolean indicator whether add initial values Stan model population_data data frame yearly population counts subnational regions. columns matching names specified year area. data frame required primary data set contains mix observations national subnational levels.

title: Fit the transition model to data. — fit_model
text: fpemplus object.

title: Fit the transition model to data. — fit_model
text: fit_model function fits transition model data. model fitted using Stan, function returns object class "fpemplus". argument runstep determines type run perform. following run steps supported: "step1a": Fit model without smoothing term, estimate longer term trends. "step1ab": Fit model smoothing terms, estimating parameters (none fixed). Unlike step1b, require prior fit estimates everything one pass. "step1b": Fit model smoothing terms, using fit step1a, estimate smoothing data model parameters. "local_national": Fit model data single country, using 1b fit. also explained documentation folder. Details hierarchical set ups used Several area-specific parameters fpemplus model hierarchical priors assigned information can shared areas. package allows structure hierarchical prior configured user hierarchical_asymptote, hierarchical_level, hierarchical_splines arguments. arguments expect character vector specifies nesting hierarchical structure. element vector must either "intercept" column name dataset, "intercept" add global intercept parameter. vector must descending order terms hierarchy: , starts "intercept" proceeds hierarchy. example, suppose fitting country-level data, dataset columns "name_country", "name_sub_region", "name_region" containing name country, sub-region, region observation belongs . specify spline coefficients fitted hierarchical model countries nested within sub-regions within regions within world, use argument hierarchical_splines = c("intercept", "name_region", "name_sub_region", "name_country"). Optionally, model parameters can fixed values previous model fit provided via global_fit argument. typical use case, global_fit fit data many geographic units (e.g., countries), current fit uses data smaller number locations. Note: remainder details currently determined runstep use global fit fix parameter values, number settings must global fit current call fpemplus: hierarchical settings, e.g. hierarchical_asymptote, hierarchical levels used parameter global fit must also used current fit. instance, global_fit used hierarchical_asymptote = c("intercept", "name_region", "name_country"), current fit valid use hierarchical_asymptote = c("intercept", "name_region", "name_country") hierarchical_asymptote = c("intercept", "name_region", "name_country", "name_subnational"), valid use hierarchical_asymptote = c("intercept", "name_region", "name_subnational"). hierarchical levels terms sigmas fix current fit contained levels hierarchy used corresponding parameters global fit. example, hierarchical_asymptote_sigmas_fixed = c("intercept", "name_region", "name_country") global fit must included least "intercept", "name_region", "name_country" hierarchical_asymptote. hierarchical levels fix current fit highest levels hierarchical structure. example, hierarchical_asymptote = c("intercept", "name_region", "name_country"), valid use hierarchical_asymptote_sigmas_fixed = c("intercept", "name_region"), valid use hierarchical_asymptote_sigmas_fixed = c("intercept", "name_country"). valid fix terms given hierarchy level without also fixing sigma estimate hierarchy level. example, specify hierarchical_asymptote_sigmas_fixed = c("intercept") hierarchical_asymptote_terms_fixed = c("intercept", "name_region") valid set fix_smoothing = TRUE also smoothing = TRUE. settings arguments model, t_star, smoothing, tau_prior, rho_prior must global_fit current fit. hierarchical_splines_sigmas_fixed hierarchical_splines_terms_fixed include hierarchical levels (.e., either different empty vector c()), settings num_knots spline_degree must global_fit current fit. geographic units appear data current fit hierarchical levels parameter fixed must also included data used global_fit. fix_nonse = TRUE, data sources appear data current fit must also included data used global_fit.

what: Get convergence diagnostics for a fitted model — get_convergence_diagnostics
title: Get convergence diagnostics for a fitted model — get_convergence_diagnostics
text: Computes saves convergence diagnostics (Rhat, ESS) hyperparameters creates density overlay plots.

title: Get convergence diagnostics for a fitted model — get_convergence_diagnostics
code: get_convergence_diagnostics(fit, model_name = "spline")

title: Get convergence diagnostics for a fitted model — get_convergence_diagnostics
text: fit fitted model object fit_model model_name Character string specifying model type. Either "spline" (checks Omega, Ptilde, Betas) "rw2" (checks Omega, gamma). Default "spline".

title: Get convergence diagnostics for a fitted model — get_convergence_diagnostics
text: NULL (invisibly). Saves diagnostics.csv diagnostics.pdf output directory.

what: Extract posterior samples of eta (latent coverage) for a specific year — get_eta_samples
title: Extract posterior samples of eta (latent coverage) for a specific year — get_eta_samples
text: Extracts posterior draws latent coverage parameter eta fitted model object specified year. validation runs, includes countries training test data.

title: Extract posterior samples of eta (latent coverage) for a specific year — get_eta_samples
code: get_eta_samples(fit, year_select = 2023)

title: Extract posterior samples of eta (latent coverage) for a specific year — get_eta_samples
text: fit fitted model object containing samples (cmdstanr draws), geo_unit_index, time_index, stan_data, data. year_select year extract eta samples. Default 2023.

title: Extract posterior samples of eta (latent coverage) for a specific year — get_eta_samples

what: Extract Innovation Samples from Fitted Model — get_inno_samples
title: Extract Innovation Samples from Fitted Model — get_inno_samples
text: Extracts epsilon_innovation (AR1 process innovations) eta posterior samples. represent temporal deviations expected coverage trajectory along underlying fitted values.

title: Extract Innovation Samples from Fitted Model — get_inno_samples
code: get_inno_samples(fit)

title: Extract Innovation Samples from Fitted Model — get_inno_samples
text: fit Fitted model object containing: samplescmdstan samples epsilon_innovation CT eta CT dataData frame iso, country, name_region, year time_indexTime index mapping columns t year geo_unit_indexGeographic unit index cluster/subcluster info

title: Extract Innovation Samples from Fitted Model — get_inno_samples
text: Nested tibble one row per (iso, year) containing: iso ISO country code year Year cluster region cluster (available) subcluster Regional subcluster (available) name_region Region name (available) draws Nested tibble draw-specific columns: draw: Posterior draw number eta: Eta parameter probit scale sd_y: Scale parameter (always 1 standardized innovations) residual: Innovation value (epsilon_innovation) level: Fitted coverage inv-probit scale level_prop: Eta proportion scale yhat: Fitted value inv-probit scale (level) y: Reconstructed observation (residual + yhat) sd_y_prop: Standard deviation proportion scale y_prop: Reconstructed observation proportion scale

title: Extract Innovation Samples from Fitted Model — get_inno_samples
text: returns innovations years within observation range country (min_obs_yr max_obs_yr).

what: Extract Residuals from Posterior Samples — get_residuals_samples

title: Extract Residuals from Posterior Samples — get_residuals_samples
text: fit Fitted model object containing: samplescmdstan samples object eta_i scale parameters stan_dataList y (observations) held_out flag

title: Extract Residuals from Posterior Samples — get_residuals_samples
text: Nested tibble one row per observation containing: obs_index Observation index y Observed value modeled scale y_prop Observed value proportion scale held_out Logical indicating observation held iso Country ISO code year Year observation cluster region cluster (available fit$data) subcluster Regional subcluster (available fit$data) name_region Region name draws Nested tibble draw-specific columns: draw: Posterior draw number yhat: Predicted mean modeled scale (= level) sd_y: Standard deviation observation modeled scale level: Level modeled (inv-probit) scale level_prop: Level proportion scale sd_y_prop: Standard deviation proportion scale y_sim: Simulated observation (validation runs )

what: Get Standard Error on invProbit Scale from Proportion — get_se_invprobitprop
title: Get Standard Error on invProbit Scale from Proportion — get_se_invprobitprop
text: Transforms standard error proportion scale invprobit scale using delta method. Uses derivative inverse probit function: SE_probit = SE_prop / dnorm(qnorm(prop))

text: prop Proportion value (0 1) se_prop Standard error proportion scale

title: Get Standard Error on invProbit Scale from Proportion — get_se_invprobitprop
text: Standard error probit scale

Default c("model1", "model2", "model3", "model4"). indicator_name Name indicator used plotting. add_estimates Boolean show model estimates/ribbons. all_survey_types Optional character vector survey types include legend (consistent legends across multiple plots). cols_sourcetypes named vector colors different data series types (e.g., DHS, MICS, etc.).

NULL, plots countries. save_plots Boolean indicator, set TRUE plots saved output directory results fit. output_folder Folder save plots , save_plots TRUE, overwrite plots saved.
